data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- When your work completes or blocks a task/roadmap milestone, leave a concise task or roadmap comment with the decisive checks, changed files, and remaining caveats. If a task has linked `roadmap_refs`, keep your report/comment explicit about the status each linked roadmap item should have so Ground or the human can keep the roadmap consistent.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- Commit your work yourself as you finish coherent pieces — `"$HORIZON_BIN" commit -m "<math-first message>" <files>` (see the horizon-commit skill). Small semantic commits (a lemma closed, a file completed) make the history and the dashboard's change view legible; don't leave one undifferentiated blob for the end-of-session sweep. Other runs may commit concurrently to the same ledger — that's fine; commit only the files you changed.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-commit — Record your own work in the workspace ledger with semantic commits via `horizon commit` — small, meaningful, math-first messages; safe under concurrent runs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- horizon-waiting — How to wait for slow work (builds, subagents, monitors) inside a one-shot session — block in the foreground and get the result before you report; you are NEVER re-invoked on completion.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge at MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0003/sessions/0017-horizon-T2

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
# Recommendation — next Ground/Horizon session

Grounded in the T2 reconcile of session `0013-horizon-T2` (RHS tilde leaf closed, `AJC.fbc` 5 → 4). Suggestions, not orders — self-scope.

- **`pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` (line 641) is the natural next FBC target.** All its named ingredients now exist — the just-closed RHS tilde leaf (`lem:coverinter_rhs_iso_tilde`), the LHS bridge, and the affine brick. The genuinely-new math is the open blueprint node `lem:coverinter_rhs_tensor_rewrite` (the corner identification `N' ≅ N ⊗_R R'`); scope a session around it.
- **The two cosimplicial `naturality` fields (lines 707, 1437) look like the cheapest wins after that.** Per the T2 report, line 1426/`twisted_cech_nerve_iso` is fully unblocked — its components (`pushPull_sigma_iso`, per-σ Beck–Chevalley) are already sorry-free; only the termwise-commuting + cosimplicial-compat assembly remains.
- **Defer `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (line 193) — it is NOT an adjointness one-liner.** Flat `g^*` is a *left* adjoint (preserves colimits), so left-exactness is genuine flatness content checked on stalks. See memory `I-0076`/`I-0078`: honest route is stalk-of-pullback infra (~400–800 LOC) or a complex-specific tilde fallback. Only its single consumer `pullback_mapHC_homologyIso` (line ~1545) needs it, so the fallback may be cheaper — decide before committing.
- **AJC build discipline still binds.** Full build ≈ 8641 jobs; the `CechSectionIdentification*` chain can cost many hours and racing builds corrupt oleans (memory `ajc-build-wall`). Check `pgrep bin/lean` + concurrent-session cwd before any `lake build`.

# Roadmap slice
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). **Beck–Chevalley heart of 02KG closed**: `openImmersion_beckChevalley`/`bareBC`, the affine essential-image member node (`Cohomology/AffinePushPullEssImage.lean`, `twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma`), and the **per-$\sigma$ RHS tilde leaf** `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde` are sorry-free and axiom-clean. Pullback along an **arbitrary** scheme morphism now preserves quasi-coherence (`Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean`, Stacks 01BG). Residual toward `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` — **4 leaf sorries**: the `pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChange` affine-gap heart (`lem:coverinter_rhs_tensor_rewrite`), the two cosimplicial `naturality` fields, and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact — **not** an adjointness freebie, see `I-0076`). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.
 tasks=T12
- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).


# Opened inbox
(no accepted inbox items)

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- **Subproject merge-back recipe (worked for T1 / Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso → AJC; reuse for Albanese / Cech back-merges).**

- **Wholesale lift beats cherry-pick** when the subproject built green on the same toolchain+mathlib rev: replace every diverged shared file with the subproject copy, add the new files, then repair AJC-only consumers. Verify first with a decl-name superset check (regex-extract `def|lemma|...` heads, `comm -23`) and a consumer grep for the decls that moved/disappeared.
- **Check for cross-module duplicate decl names BEFORE building**: two modules both imported by the root that declare the same fully-qualified name are a fatal clash. In T1, LBC's `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` had absorbed 13 decls of AJC's `PullbackTensorComp.lean` (incl. `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`) → the right fix was to **retire the superseded module** (its 8 unique helpers had no consumers).
- **Blueprint: port per-chapter, not per-project.** Wholesale-copy a chapter only after a comment-stripped cross-ref check: (refs of incoming chapter) ⊆ (its labels ∪ other AJC chapters' labels), and no other AJC chapter references a disappearing label. In T1 the TOS chapter ported cleanly; LBC's restructured `RelPicFunctor`/`LineBundlePullback` chapters did NOT (6 `\cref` sites in `Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex` would dangle) — kept AJC's copies. Their reconciliation is optional future work.
- Big-file build times on this box: `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` (4592 L) + `TensorObjInverse.lean` (3424 L) + rest of the cone ≈ 15 min wall with warm mathlib cache.
- FBC bareBC Stage-2 patterns + scratch-iteration gotchas (session 0003-T2)

**Mate-factorization pattern (landed, reusable for other Beck-Chevalley mates):**

- `mateEquiv adj₁ adj₂ α |>.app c` factors as `adj₂.unit.app _ ≫ R₂.map (α.app _) ≫ R₂.map (H.map (adj₁.counit.app c))`. Proof: `simp [<def>, mateEquiv_apply]` then `erw [Category.id_comp, Category.id_comp, Category.comp_id]; rfl` (three defeq-blocked `𝟙`s + one defeq head).
- Counit of `pullback q ⊣ pushforward q` at open immersion `q` is iso: `IsIso.of_isIso_fac_left (Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_hom_app_counit (restrictAdjunction q) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction q) c)` with `haveI` on `(restrictAdjunction q).counit` iso via `inferInstanceAs (IsIso (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso q).hom)`.
- essImage → unit-iso: `Adjunction.isIso_unit_app_of_iso` (needs `pushforward` Full+Faithful — Mathlib instances for open immersions). Reverse `mem_essImage_of_unit_isIso`.
- **essImage of `p'_*` is cover-local** (`essImage_pushforward_of_openCover`, now in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`): site-level `restrictAdjunction` unit has plain `M.presheaf.map homOfLE` components; `isIso_iff_isIso_restrict` + `Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` need NO push/pull coherence — components of the restricted map are again plain presheaf maps; match via `w''(O ⊓ w⁻¹R) = R ⊓ w''O` (`Set.image_inter_preimage`) + one `eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h)` factor. Poset-hom equalities: `congr 1` after `rw [← Functor.map_comp]` discharges by proof-irrelevance; op-level casts via `Quiver.Hom.unop_inj; Subsingleton.elim`.

**Scratch-iteration gotchas:**

- `lake env lean scratch.lean` does NOT apply lakefile `leanOptions`. Add `set_option maxSynthPendingDepth 3` at scratch top or instance chains through functor categories fail mysteriously (e.g. `IsIso ((leftAdjointUniq ..).hom.app M)` composites). Destination files under `lake build` are fine.
- Mathlib-only lemmas iterate in ~3 min via a root-level scratch importing `Mathlib` (+ light project modules whose oleans exist) — 20x faster than the project file. Delete scratch before commit.
- `CechSectionIdentificationLegMid1.lean` takes ~70+ min to compile (`maxHeartbeats 0`, documented in-file); it IS in `CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional`'s import cone (via `CechTermAcyclic → CechSectionIdentificationBase → … `). Full verification of Unconditional edits costs hours if its olean is stale/missing.
- Beware RACING concurrent lake builds of the same project from leftover/parallel sessions (two `lean` processes on the same file observed) — olean corruption risk, duplicated 70-min work.
- Recipe: Stacks 00OE/00TT closed-point regularity for standard-smooth algebras (v4.31 Mathlib suffices)

**The smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula at maximal ideals needs NO transcendence-degree theory** — do not build Noether-normalisation dimension theory for it. Proven route (Albanese `StandardSmoothDimension.lean` + `CodimOneExtension.lean` §3.B, all axiom-clean):

- `ht M = #ι` for maximal `M ⊆ MvPolynomial ι k`: `Finite.induction_empty_option` (works with hypotheses depending on `ι` — the tactic auto-generalises `M`, `hM`); step = `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` (contraction maximal, `MvPolynomial.isJacobsonRing` instance exists for `Finite ι`) + `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`; transport via `RingEquiv.height_map/height_comap` + `Ideal.map/comap_isMaximal_of_equiv`.
- Lower bound `n ≤ ht m` in standard-smooth `S`: pull `m` back along `P.algebraMap_surjective` of the submersive presentation; `Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le` (Krull height thm, relative form) + `spanFinrank(ker) ≤ #σ` via `P.span_range_relation_eq_ker`; quotient side identified with `m` via `RingHom.quotientKerEquivOfSurjective_apply_mk` + `Ideal.map_comap_of_surjective`; cancel in ℕ∞ with `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right`.
- Upper bound is free: `IsRegularLocalRing.of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le` + `IsLocalRing.spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- k̄-rationality of maximal ideals: `finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing` (Zariski) + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral` + `Ideal.bijective_algebraMap_quotient_residueField`. **Trap:** install `letI := Ideal.Quotient.field m` BEFORE any other `haveI` on `S ⧸ m` (FiniteType, Module.Finite) — installing it after poisons `Algebra k (S ⧸ m)` synthesis (semiring-key mismatch; `respectTransparency false` does NOT fix it; Field-first ordering does).

**SUPERSEDED for the all-points case (run 0006 T6): Stacks 00OF is NO LONGER needed.** The old claim here — "remaining genuine gap for all-points `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` is Stacks 00OF, do not attempt" — is now false. See [[I-0047]]: `Albanese/SmoothPrimeRegularity.lean` proves regularity at EVERY prime of a standard-smooth algebra over a perfect field, Serre-free (trdeg–height induction via Stacks 00ON + conormal split-exactness from `FormallySmooth.of_perfectField`), so `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` / `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` are now axiom-clean. This closed-point recipe is retained only for its k̄-rationality trap and the maximal-ideal API details.
- **FBC member-node patterns (session 0007-T2) — the essImage route that closed `IsIso bareBC`.**

13406	[RHS abstract \(\to\) tilde for a single intersection open (base-changed side)]
13407	  \label{lem:coverinter_rhs_iso_tilde}
13408	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj_coverInter_baseChanged_pushforward_iso_tilde}
13409	  \uses{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma, lem:coverinter_lhs_iso_tilde,

13413	  \(f : X \to \operatorname{Spec} R\) be \emph{separated} over the affine base
13414	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), let \(g : \operatorname{Spec} R' \to \operatorname{Spec} R\) be the
13415	  base-change morphism, and let \(f' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\) be the \emph{separated}
13416	  (\([\,\mathrm{IsSeparated}\ f'\,]\)) pulled-back morphism with \(g' : X' \to X\) the top map of
13417	  the cartesian square. Assume the pulled-back affine open-immersion cover
13418	  \(\mathfrak{U}' = \{\mathfrak{U}'.X\, i\}\) of \(X'\) consists of affine opens
13419	  (\([\,\forall i,\,\mathrm{IsAffine}(\mathfrak{U}'.X\,i)\,]\)). Fix the affine intersection
13420	  open \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A_\sigma\) (\cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}) with
13421	  \(j_\sigma : V \hookrightarrow X\) and \(N = \Gamma(V, (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F})\); let
13422	  \(j'_\sigma : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) be the pullback of \(j_\sigma\) along \(g'\), so that
13423	  \(V' = X' \times_X V = \operatorname{Spec}(A_\sigma \otimes_R R')\), and set
13424	  \[
13425	    N' \;=\; \Gamma\bigl(V', (j'_\sigma)^*((g')^*\mathcal{F})\bigr)
13426	  \]
13427	  for the raw base-changed corner module. Write \(\psi : R' \to A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\) for the
13428	  ring map presenting the composite \(f' \circ j'_\sigma : V' \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\) as
13429	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\psi\). Then there is an isomorphism of
13430	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R'}\)-modules
13431	  \[
13432	    f'_*\Bigl((g')^*\bigl(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,
13433	      (\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma)\bigr)\Bigr)
13434	      \;\cong\; (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\,\widetilde{N'}.
13435	  \]
13436	  (The further identification \(N' \cong N \otimes_R R'\) is
13437	  \cref{lem:coverinter_rhs_tensor_rewrite}.)
13438	\end{lemma}
13439	\begin{proof}

13444	        def:push_pull_obj}
13445	  First reorganise the pulled-back data by the per-intersection-open \(X\)-level
13446	  Beck--Chevalley isomorphism \cref{lem:twisted_cech_nerve_per_sigma}: since \(j_\sigma\) is an
13447	  open immersion whose pullback along \(g'\) is the open immersion
13448	  \(j'_\sigma : V' \hookrightarrow X'\) onto the preimage \((g')^{-1}(V)\),
13449	  \[
13450	    (g')^*\bigl(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma)\bigr)
13451	      = (g')^*\bigl((j_\sigma)_*(j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
13452	      \;\cong\; (j'_\sigma)_*(j'_\sigma)^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
13453	      = \operatorname{pushPullObj}\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\,(\operatorname{Over.mk}
13454	        j'_\sigma).
13455	  \]
13456	  Pushing forward along \(f'\) reduces the left side of the claim to
13457	  \(f'_*\bigl(\operatorname{pushPullObj}((g')^*\mathcal{F})\,(\operatorname{Over.mk}
13458	  j'_\sigma)\bigr)\). The base-changed module \((g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent by
13459	  \cref{lem:pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom}, and the intersection open
13460	  \(V' = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}'\,\sigma\) of the base-changed cover is
13461	  affine for the separated \(f' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\) over the affine base
13462	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R'\) (\cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine}). Hence the LHS-side bridge
13463	  \cref{lem:coverinter_lhs_iso_tilde}, applied to the base-changed data
13464	  \((f', \mathfrak{U}', (g')^*\mathcal{F})\) at the index tuple \(\sigma\), gives
13465	  \[

13467	      j'_\sigma)\bigr)
13468	      \;\cong\; (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\,\widetilde{N'},
13469	    \qquad N' = \Gamma\bigl(V', (j'_\sigma)^*((g')^*\mathcal{F})\bigr),
13470	  \]
13471	  with \(\psi = \Gamma(f' \circ j'_\sigma) : R' \to A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\). This is the asserted
13472	  isomorphism.

13493	        lem:openimm_beckchevalley}

13497	  identification \((j'_\sigma)^*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \cong

13499	  in \cref{lem:openimm_beckchevalley}), where \(g'_V : V' \to V\) is the top map of that square.

13501	  is quasi-coherent, \(\cong \widetilde{N}\). The top map \(g'_V\) is the base change of the
13502	  base-change morphism \(g\) over the affine \(V\), hence the affine morphism
13503	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\rho\) for the corner ring map \(\rho : A_\sigma \to A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\);
13504	  pulling \(\widetilde{N}\) back along it is, by the pullback tilde dictionary
13505	  \cref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso},

13507	  = \widetilde{N \otimes_R R'}\). Taking global sections over
13508	  \(V' = \operatorname{Spec}(A_\sigma \otimes_R R')\) gives
13509	  \(N' \cong N \otimes_{A_\sigma}(A_\sigma \otimes_R R') = N \otimes_R R'\), naturally in

13512	  \(B = A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\)) is the pure algebraic core
13513	  \cref{lem:coverinter_baseChanged_module_iso_tensor}. A consumer rewrites the right-hand side
13514	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\widetilde{N'}\) as
13515	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\widetilde{N \otimes_R R'}\) along this identification.

13528	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be \emph{separated} with affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec} R\), let
13529	  \(g : S' \to S\) be a base-change morphism with \(S' = \operatorname{Spec} R'\) affine, and
13530	  let
13531	  \[
13532	    \begin{array}{ccc}
13533	      X' & \xrightarrow{\ g'\ } & X\\
13534	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f}\\
13535	      S' & \xrightarrow{\ g\ } & S
13536	    \end{array}
13537	  \]
13538	  be the associated cartesian square. Fix an affine open-immersion cover
13539	  \(\mathfrak{U} = \{U_i = \operatorname{Spec} A_i\}\) of \(X\) and a multi-index
13540	  \(\sigma : \kappa \to I\), with intersection open
13541	  \(V = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathfrak{U}\,\sigma = \bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\) and open
13542	  immersion \(j_\sigma : V \hookrightarrow X\). Then there is an isomorphism of
13543	  \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules

13545	    g^*\Bigl(f_*\bigl((j_\sigma)_* (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\Bigr)
13546	      \;\cong\;
13547	    f'_*\Bigl((g')^*\bigl((j_\sigma)_* (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\Bigr),
13548	  \]
13549	  i.e.\ \(g^*\bigl(f_*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk}
13550	  j_\sigma))\bigr) \cong f'_*\bigl((g')^*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,
13551	  (\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma))\bigr)\). This is the per-intersection-open instance of the
13552	  Beck--Chevalley comparison; it is the genuine open content that survives the
13553	  coproduct/product decomposition of the degreewise {\v C}ech nerve.

13555	\begin{proof}
13556	  \uses{lem:coverinter_lhs_iso_tilde, lem:coverinter_rhs_iso_tilde,

13559	        lem:isAffineOpen_biInf_mathlib, lem:cech_interopen_isaffine,
13560	        lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout, lem:commRingCat_isPushout_iff_mathlib}
13561	  % NOTE: iter-325 partial state — step (2) (LHS → tilde) is LANDED in-file via the new
13562	  % sorry-free axiom-clean bridge `pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde_affine`
13563	  % (\cref{lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde_affine}): the heart's LHS half type-checks. The residual
13564	  % `sorry` is steps (3)+(4) — the RHS-→-tilde leaf `lem:coverinter_rhs_iso_tilde`, itself gated
13565	  % on the X-level open-immersion Beck–Chevalley leaf `lem:openimm_beckchevalley`. See its
13566	  % blueprint sketch for the stalkwise route that bypasses the DEAD adjoint-mate approach.
13567	  The two sides of the comparison are routed independently to the common affine
13568	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\cdot)_*\widetilde{(-)}\) form, where the affine termwise base change
13569	  closes the gap between them.
13570	  \begin{enumerate}
13571	    \item \emph{\(V\) is affine.} The intersection open
13572	      \(V = \bigcap_k U_{\sigma(k)}\) is a finite nonempty intersection of affine opens of the
13573	      separated scheme \(X\), hence affine by \cref{lem:cech_interopen_isaffine} (the
13574	      separated-diagonal instance of \cref{lem:isAffineOpen_biInf_mathlib}),
13575	      \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A_\sigma\); write \(\varphi : R \to A_\sigma\) for the ring map
13576	      presenting \(f \circ j_\sigma\) as \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) and
13577	      \(N = \Gamma(V, (j_\sigma)^*\mathcal{F})\).
13578	    \item \emph{LHS \(\to\) tilde.} By \cref{lem:coverinter_lhs_iso_tilde} the left abstract
13579	      term is the affine pushforward of \(\widetilde{N}\) over \(S\),
13580	      \[
13581	        f_*\bigl(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma)\bigr)
13582	          \;\cong\; (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\,\widetilde{N},
13583	      \]
13584	      so applying \(g^*\) gives
13585	      \(g^*\bigl(f_*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk}
13586	      j_\sigma))\bigr) \cong g^*\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{N}\bigr)\).
13587	    \item \emph{RHS \(\to\) tilde.} By \cref{lem:coverinter_rhs_iso_tilde} the right abstract
13588	      term is likewise the affine pushforward of the base-changed module
13589	      \(N \otimes_R R'\) over \(S'\),
13590	      \[
13591	        f'_*\Bigl((g')^*\bigl(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,
13592	          (\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma)\bigr)\Bigr)
13593	          \;\cong\; (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\,\widetilde{N \otimes_R R'},
13594	      \]
13595	      where \(\psi : R' \to A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\) presents
13596	      \(f' \circ j'_\sigma\). Both comparison sides are now in
13597	      \((\operatorname{Spec}\cdot)_*\widetilde{(-)}\) form.
13598	    \item \emph{The affine pushout square closes the gap.} Restrict the cartesian base-change
13599	      square over \(j_\sigma : V \hookrightarrow X\). By
13600	      \cref{lem:restricted_cartesian_affine_pushout} the resulting square
13601	      \(X' \times_X V \to V\), \(X' \times_X V \to X'\), \(g'\), \(j_\sigma\) is cartesian, and
13602	      with \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A_\sigma\) (step~1), \(S = \operatorname{Spec} R\),
13603	      \(S' = \operatorname{Spec} R'\) affine it is a cartesian square of affine schemes,
13604	      \(\operatorname{Spec}(A_\sigma \otimes_R R') \to \operatorname{Spec} A_\sigma\),
13605	      \(\operatorname{Spec}(A_\sigma \otimes_R R') \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\). Applying global
13606	      sections, the affine-pullback \(\leftrightarrow\) ring-pushout equivalence
13607	      \cref{lem:commRingCat_isPushout_iff_mathlib} converts it into the cocartesian square of
13608	      commutative rings
13609	      \[
13610	        \begin{array}{ccc}
13611	          R & \xrightarrow{\ \varphi\ } & A_\sigma\\
13612	          \big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow\\
13613	          R' & \xrightarrow{\ \psi\ } & A_\sigma \otimes_R R'
13614	        \end{array}
13615	      \]
13616	      with corner \(A_\sigma \otimes_R R'\). For this pushout square the affine termwise base
13617	      change \cref{lem:cech_degree_affine_baseChange}, the per-open instance of
13618	      \cref{lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange}, supplies
13619	      \[
13620	        g^*\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*\widetilde{N}\bigr)
13621	          \;\cong\; (\operatorname{Spec}\psi)_*\,\widetilde{N \otimes_R R'}.
13622	      \]
13623	      Composing step~2 (and \(g^*\)), this affine isomorphism, and the inverse of step~3 yields
13624	      the asserted comparison
13625	      \(g^*\bigl(f_*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,(\operatorname{Over.mk}
13626	      j_\sigma))\bigr) \cong f'_*\bigl((g')^*(\operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,
13627	      (\operatorname{Over.mk} j_\sigma))\bigr)\).
13628	  \end{enumerate}
13629	\end{proof}

334	/-- **Degreewise affine reduction of the Čech base change** (Stacks 02KG; shared sub-lemma).
335	Fix a cosimplicial degree `p`. On the standard affine model of the cover the `(p+1)`-fold
336	fibre power `U_{i₀…iₚ} = U_{i₀} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{iₚ}` is `Spec` of the finite affine intersection
337	`A := A_{i₀} ⊗_R ⋯ ⊗_R A_{iₚ}` of the coordinate rings of the cover members, and the X-level
338	cartesian square defining the base change along `g'` restricts, over `U_{i₀…iₚ}`, to the affine
339	pushout (tensor) square of rings `(φ : R ⟶ A, ψ : R ⟶ R', ρ : A ⟶ B, σ : R' ⟶ B)`. On that
340	affine model the degreewise Beck–Chevalley comparison
341	```
342	  g'^*(p_* p^* F)  ≅  p'_* p'^*(g'^* F)        over U_{i₀…iₚ}
343	```
344	IS the affine termwise base change `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange`
345	(`FlatBaseChange.lean`), assembled from the concrete tilde dictionaries
346	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the commutative-algebra
347	cancellation `cancelBaseChange` — *not* the canonical adjoint mate `pushforwardBaseChangeMap`.
348	These affine identifications are natural with respect to the index-omission maps that generate
349	the cosimplicial structure of the nerve (each coface is the ring inclusion that inserts the
350	omitted tensor factor), since `affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange` is natural in the ring.
351	
352	This is the **shared per-degree brick** consumed by both
353	`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso` and `twisted_cech_nerve_iso`: at each degree, after the
354	affine identification of the fibre power, the `app` field of either leaf is this isomorphism.
355	Sorry-free: a direct repackaging of the affine termwise base change at the intersection ring
356	`A`. Project-local; see blueprint `lem:cech_degree_affine_baseChange`. -/
357	noncomputable def cech_degree_affine_baseChange {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
358	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
359	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
360	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ψ)).obj
361	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
362	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map σ)).obj
363	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map ρ)).obj (tilde M)) :=
364	  affinePushforwardPullbackBaseChange φ ψ ρ σ h M

366	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the abstract→affine `pushPullObj ≅ tilde` bridge
367	
368	The two degreewise Beck–Chevalley leaves (`cech_pushforward_baseChange_natIso`,
369	`twisted_cech_nerve_iso`) reduce, on the affine model of the cover, to the sorry-free affine
370	brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`. The missing edge (Stacks 01I8 / 01BG) is the identification
371	of the abstract push–pull data of a fibre power with the `tilde`-model over `Spec`. We build it at
372	the two well-typed altitudes flagged in `analogies/fbc-pushpull-tilde-317.md`:
373	
374	* **altitude 1** (`pullbackRestrict_iso_tilde`): the restriction `p^* F = (V.ι)^* F` of a
375	  quasi-coherent `F` to an affine open `V` of `X`, *pushed forward along the whole-scheme iso*
376	  `V ≅ Spec Γ(X, V)` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`), is `tilde` of its global sections over
377	  `Spec Γ(X, V)`. Quasi-coherence is preserved by pullback along the open immersion `V.ι`
378	  (`pullback_isQuasicoherent`/`isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`) and by pushforward along the
379	  iso `isoSpec` (`pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh`), so the affine structure theorem 01I8
380	  (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, unconditional via the live instance
381	  `isIso_fromTildeΓ_of_quasicoherent`) applies.
382	* **altitude 2** (`pushPullObj_pushforward_iso_tilde`): over the affine base `S = Spec R`, the
383	  pushed-forward push–pull object `f_*(p_* p^* F) = f_*((V.ι)_* (V.ι)^* F)` is `(Spec φ)_* (tilde N)`
384	  — collapse `f_* ∘ (V.ι)_*` to `(V.ι ≫ f)_*` by `pushforwardComp`, factor
385	  `V.ι ≫ f = isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map φ` (with `φ := Spec.preimage (fromSpec ≫ f)`,
386	  `Spec.map_preimage`), split off `(Spec.map φ)_*` by `pushforwardComp` again, and feed altitude 1
387	  through `(Spec.map φ)_*`. This is exactly the form the brick `cech_degree_affine_baseChange`
388	  consumes. See blueprint `lem:pullback_preserves_quasicoherent`, `lem:pushPullObj_iso_tilde`.

390	All ingredients are axiom-clean project infrastructure: `isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens`
391	(`CechTermAcyclic`), `pushforward_iso_preserves_qcoh` (`OpenImmersionPushforward`),
392	`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections` (`QcohTildeSections`, the unconditional 01I8 structure theorem).

396	quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the restriction `(V.ι)^* F` is quasi-coherent on `V`.  This is the

405	  isQuasicoherent_pullback_opens V F hF

407	/-- **Altitude 1 of the bridge: `(V.ι)^* F` pushed to `Spec Γ(X,V)` is `tilde N`** (Stacks 01I8).

444	        (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj
445	          ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward hV.isoSpec.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback V.ι).obj F)))) :=
446	  have heq : V.ι ≫ f = hV.isoSpec.hom ≫ Spec.map (Spec.preimage (hV.fromSpec ≫ f)) := by

477	          (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj

483	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp f S.isoSpec.hom).symm.app
484	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
485	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp S.isoSpec.hom S.isoSpec.inv).app
486	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
487	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr S.isoSpec.hom_inv_id).app
488	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj (pushPullObj F (Over.mk V.ι))) ≪≫
489	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId S).app

714	mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence). The affine-local brick is the floor of that construction:
715	over the fully affine pushout square
716	```
717	  Spec (A ⊗_R R') --Spec ρ--> Spec A
718	  |Spec σ                      |Spec φ
719	  v                            v
720	  Spec R'      ----Spec ψ---->  Spec R
721	```
722	(`φ : R → A`, `ψ : R → R'`, `ρ = includeLeft`, `σ = includeRight` into `A ⊗_R R'`) it produces the
723	termwise isomorphism `g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)`, assembled from the two sorry-free tilde
724	dictionaries `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` and the pure
725	commutative-algebra cancellation `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange`, never forming the adjoint
726	mate.
727	
728	STATUS (iter-305): the load-bearing module-level core — the `R'`-linear iso
729	`(extendScalars ψ) ∘ (restrictScalars φ) ≅ (restrictScalars σ) ∘ (extendScalars ρ)` realising
730	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ (A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` — was PROVEN to typecheck axiom-clean in the **CommRingCat framing**
731	(ambient algebras supplied as `φ.hom.toAlgebra` / `ψ.hom.toAlgebra`), by `@`-threading the
732	`RingHom.toAlgebra` instances through `Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` (so its output is defeq to
733	the `compHom`-based `ModuleCat.extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects) with `IsPushout` transported
734	from `TensorProduct.isPushout'` via `Algebra.algebra_ext`. See `task_results` for the full working
735	construction. It is NOT yet landed as a top-level declaration: stating the tensor `A ⊗_R R'` in the
736	signature forces a Type-u framing whose *opaque* ambient `[Algebra R A]`/`[Algebra R R']` lets the
737	GLOBAL `Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra`/`rightAlgebra` instances win over the `toAlgebra` `letI`s,
738	breaking the `compHom`-defeq on the inner `Module A (A⊗_R R')` and outer `Module R' (·)`. The
739	recommended fix (iter-306) is the abstract-`B` framing: take `ρ : A ⟶ B`, `σ : R' ⟶ B` and a
740	`CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ` (or `IsPullback` of the Spec square) hypothesis, derive
741	`Algebra.IsPushout` in the proof with all algebras `toAlgebra` (no global tensor instances to shadow),
742	and assemble the 5-step chain. See blueprint `lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/
743	
744	/-- **Module-level base-change cancellation iso (abstract-`B` framing).** Given a pushout square of
745	commutative rings
746	```
747	  R --φ--> A
748	  |ψ       |ρ
749	  v        v
750	  R' --σ-> B
751	```
752	(so `B ≅ A ⊗_R R'`) and an `A`-module `M`, the `R'`-linear isomorphism
753	`extendScalars_ψ (restrictScalars_φ M) ≅ restrictScalars_σ (extendScalars_ρ M)`, i.e.
754	`R' ⊗_R M ≅ restrictScalars_σ (B ⊗_A M)`. It realises the inverse of Mathlib's
755	`Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange` between the extension-of-scalars objects attached to the two
756	legs of the square. The abstract `B` (rather than the concrete `A ⊗_R R'`) keeps all ambient
757	algebra instances `toAlgebra`, so no global tensor-product instance shadows them and
758	`cancelBaseChange`'s output module structures land defeq on the `compHom`-based
759	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` objects. Project-local: the module core of the affine termwise
760	base-change brick. See blueprint `lem:baseChangeCancelModuleIso`. -/
761	noncomputable def baseChangeCancelModuleIso {R A R' B : CommRingCat.{u}}
762	    (φ : R ⟶ A) (ψ : R ⟶ R') (ρ : A ⟶ B) (σ : R' ⟶ B)
763	    (h : CategoryTheory.IsPushout φ ψ ρ σ) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :
764	    (ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) ≅
765	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars σ.hom).obj ((ModuleCat.extendScalars ρ.hom).obj M) := by
766	  letI iRA : Algebra (R : Type u) (A : Type u) := φ.hom.toAlgebra
767	  letI iRR' : Algebra (R : Type u) (R' : Type u) := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
768	  letI iAB : Algebra (A : Type u) (B : Type u) := ρ.hom.toAlgebra
769	  letI iR'B : Algebra (R' : Type u) (B : Type u) := σ.hom.toAlgebra
770	  letI iRB : Algebra (R : Type u) (B : Type u) := (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom).toAlgebra
771	  letI iMR : Module (R : Type u) M := ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M).isModule
772	  haveI tRAM : IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A M :=
773	    ⟨fun r a m => by rw [Algebra.smul_def, mul_smul]; rfl⟩
774	  haveI tRAB : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) A B _ iAB.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
775	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) A B _ _ _ iRA iAB iRB (fun _ => rfl)
776	  haveI tRR'B : @IsScalarTower (R : Type u) R' B _ iR'B.toSMul iRB.toSMul :=
777	    @IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq (R : Type u) R' B _ _ _ iRR' iR'B iRB (fun r => by
778	      change (ρ.hom.comp φ.hom) r = σ.hom (ψ.hom r)
779	      have := congrArg (fun m => (CommRingCat.Hom.hom m) r) h.w
780	      simpa using this)
781	  haveI hpush : @Algebra.IsPushout (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iR'B iAB iRB tRAB tRR'B :=
782	    (CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout).mp h.flip
783	  exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso
784	    (@Algebra.IsPushout.cancelBaseChange (R : Type u) R' _ _ iRR' A B _ _ iRA iRB iAB iR'B
785	      tRAB tRR'B hpush M _ iMR _ tRAM).symm
786	
787	/-- **Affine termwise base change (abstract-`B` framing).** For a pushout square of commutative
788	rings `(φ : R ⟶ A, ψ : R ⟶ R', ρ : A ⟶ B, σ : R' ⟶ B)` — equivalently the cartesian square of
789	affine schemes obtained by `Spec` — and an `A`-module `M`, the base-change comparison
790	`g^*(f_* M̃) ≅ f'_*((g')^* M̃)` for `f = Spec φ`, `g = Spec ψ`, `f' = Spec σ`, `g' = Spec ρ`. It is
791	assembled as a 5-step chain from the two affine tilde dictionaries
792	(`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso`) and the module-level cancellation core
793	`baseChangeCancelModuleIso`, and it **never** forms the adjoint mate
794	`pushforwardBaseChangeMap`, so it sidesteps the unresolved mate ↔ `cancelBaseChange` coherence
795	obligation of the canonical route. This is the affine-local brick (the floor) of the active
796	concrete-tilde route to flat base change (Stacks 02KG/02KH). Project-local. See blueprint
797	`lem:affine_pushforward_pullback_baseChange`. -/

name = "AlgebraicJacobian"
version = "0.1.0"
keywords = ["math"]
defaultTargets = ["AlgebraicJacobian"]
packagesDir = "../../.lake-packages"


[leanOptions]

relaxedAutoImplicit = false
weak.linter.mathlibStandardSet = true
maxSynthPendingDepth = 3

git = "https://github.com/PatrickMassot/checkdecls.git"

git = "https://github.com/leanprover/doc-gen4"
# pinned to the doc-gen4 commit on main that targets toolchain v4.31.0
# (rev "main" tracks v4.32.0-rc1 and conflicts with mathlib v4.31.0)
rev = "0bc516c1b9db"

# mathlib required LAST so its transitive dep versions (e.g. Cli) take
# precedence over doc-gen4's, keeping `lake exe cache get` hashes correct.
[[require]]
name = "mathlib"
scope = "leanprover-community"
rev = "v4.31.0"

[[lean_lib]]
name = "AlgebraicJacobian"

Qq
aesop
batteries
importGraph
plausible
proofwidgets

open TopCat AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace CategoryTheory Opposite

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open _root_.PrimeSpectrum

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules. -/
def modulesSpecToSheaf :
    (Spec R).Modules ⥤ TopCat.Sheaf (ModuleCat R) (Spec R) :=
  SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (.op ⊤)
    (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop) ⋙
  sheafCompose _ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom)

/-- The global section functor for `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules -/
noncomputable
def moduleSpecΓFunctor : (Spec (.of R)).Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R :=
  modulesSpecToSheaf ⋙ TopCat.Sheaf.forget _ _ ⋙ (evaluation _ _).obj (.op ⊤)

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
open PrimeSpectrum in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪_{Spec R}` modules to sheaves of `R`-modules is fully faithful. -/
def SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful : (modulesSpecToSheaf (R := R)).FullyFaithful where
  preimage {M N} f := ⟨fun U ↦ ModuleCat.ofHom ⟨(f.1.app U).hom.toAddHom, by
    intro t m
    apply TopCat.Presheaf.IsSheaf.section_ext (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).2
    intro x hxU

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

/-- **Unconditional higher direct image via Čech.** For a separated quasi-compact
`f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X`, and a quasi-coherent
`F : X.Modules`, the `i`-th higher direct image is the `i`-th cohomology of the
relative Čech complex. This needs **no** enough-injectives hypothesis on
`O_X`-modules: it is the cohomology of an explicit complex of quasi-coherent
sheaves. By `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` it agrees with the derived-functor
higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and is independent of the
chosen affine cover up to canonical isomorphism. For `i = 0` one recovers the
ordinary pushforward `R⁰ f_* F = f_* F`. -/
noncomputable def cechHigherDirectImage (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover)

-/

universe u

open CategoryTheory Limits

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

open Scheme.Modules

/-! ## Pullback along an iso is pushforward along the inverse -/

/-- **Pullback along a scheme isomorphism is pushforward along the inverse.**  For
`e : X ≅ Y`, the pushforward `(e.inv)_*` is quasi-inverse to `(e.hom)_*`
(`pushforwardEquivOfIso`), hence left adjoint to it; by uniqueness of left adjoints it is
isomorphic to `(e.hom)^*`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_bareBC_app_isIso_affine`
(supporting piece). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pullbackIsoPushforwardInv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (e : X ≅ Y) :
    Scheme.Modules.pullback e.hom ≅ Scheme.Modules.pushforward e.inv :=
  Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq
    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction e.hom)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso e).symm.toAdjunction

/-! ## Open-open pushforward–restriction commutation

For opens `V₀, U` of `X`, restricting a pushforward `(V₀.ι)_* K` to `U` agrees with pushing
the restriction of `K` to `U ∩ V₀` forward along `(U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι`.  All four functors are
site-level pushforwards; both composites are pushforwards along the SAME opens functor, so
the comparison is the `restrictFunctorComp`/`glueOverlapBaseChangeIso` pattern. -/

variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

/-- The range of `(U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι` is contained in `V₀`. -/
lemma range_opensInter_le (V₀ U : X.Opens) :
    Set.range ((U.ι ⁻¹ᵁ V₀).ι ≫ U.ι) ⊆ Set.range V₀.ι := by
  simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, TopCat.coe_comp, Set.range_comp, Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
====

The pushforward-of-an-iso construction is generic in the scheme isomorphism `φ : X ≅ Y`. -/

/-- **A scheme isomorphism induces an equivalence of module categories.** For `φ : X ≅ Y`, the
pushforward `Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom` is an equivalence with quasi-inverse the pushforward
along `φ.inv`; the unit/counit are supplied by the
`pushforwardId`/`pushforwardComp`/`pushforwardCongr` coherences. Project-local: Mathlib packages the
pushforward functor and its coherences but not the equivalence assembled from an iso (the genuine
equivalence used to transport `Ext` for Need #1). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
    X.Modules ≌ Y.Modules :=
  CategoryTheory.Equivalence.mk
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom) (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.inv)
    ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).symm ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.hom_inv_id.symm ≪≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.hom φ.inv).symm)
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp φ.inv φ.hom ≪≫ Scheme.Modules.pushforwardCongr φ.inv_hom_id ≪≫
      Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId Y)

/-- The functor of `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` is (defeq) the additive `pushforward φ.hom`. -/
noncomputable instance pushforwardEquivOfIso_functor_additive {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y) :
    (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.Additive :=
  inferInstanceAs (Scheme.Modules.pushforward φ.hom).Additive

/-- **`Ext` transport along a scheme isomorphism.** For `φ : X ≅ Y` and `A B : X.Modules`, the
equivalence `pushforwardEquivOfIso φ` (whose functor is fully faithful, exact, and injective-object
preserving) upgrades the additive map `Ext.mapExactFunctor` to an isomorphism of `Ext`-groups
`Ext A B n ≃+ Ext (φ_* A) (φ_* B) n`, via `Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects`.
Project-local: the `Ext`-isomorphism half of Need #1 (the spectrum-equivalence transport). -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Modules.pushforwardExtAddEquiv {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (φ : X ≅ Y)
    [EnoughInjectives X.Modules] (A B : X.Modules) (n : ℕ) :
    CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext A B n ≃+
      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj A)
        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.obj B) n :=
  AddEquiv.ofBijective ((Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor.mapExtAddHom A B n)
    (Functor.mapExt_bijective_of_preservesInjectiveObjects
      (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardEquivOfIso φ).functor A B n)

/-- **Need #1 — `Ext` transport along the whole-scheme spectrum equivalence.** For an affine scheme
`U`, the canonical iso `U ≅ Spec Γ(U,𝒪)` (`Scheme.isoSpec`) induces, for every `q`, an isomorphism
`Ext^q_U(A, B) ≅ Ext^q_{Spec Γ U}(φ_* A, φ_* B)`. In particular the residual `Ext` over `U` vanishes
iff its transport over `Spec Γ U` does. Project-local target of `lem:modules_isoSpec_ext_transport`.

theorem coverInterOpen_baseChange_eq (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
    (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} [Finite κ] (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
    coverInterOpen ((Scheme.Pullback.openCoverOfLeft 𝒰 f g).pushforwardIso h.isoPullback.symm.hom) σ
      = g' ⁻¹ᵁ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ := by
  apply TopologicalSpace.Opens.ext
  rw [coverInterOpen, coverInterOpen, coe_iInf_of_finite, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe,
    coe_iInf_of_finite, Set.preimage_iInter]
  refine Set.iInter_congr fun k => ?_
  have hk := coverOpen_baseChange_eq f g f' g' h 𝒰 (σ k)
  simp only [coverOpen]
  rw [hk, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe]

/-- **Bare Beck–Chevalley mate** for the restricted cartesian square `IsPullback gV p' p g'`
(`gV ≫ p = p' ≫ g'`).  This is the canonical base-change natural transformation
`g'^* ∘ p_* ⟶ p'_* ∘ gV^*` obtained as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform) — under the
`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions for `p` and `p'` — of the canonical pullback 2-isomorphism
`pullback g' ⋙ pullback p' ≅ pullback p ⋙ pullback gV` coming from `p' ≫ g' = gV ≫ p`.

This natural transformation always exists (no flatness, no open-immersion hypothesis): it is the
*comparison map* whose being an iso is the genuine Beck–Chevalley content.  It is a 6-line local
restatement of the (sorry-tainted-`QuotScheme`) `canonicalBaseChangeMap` so that this file does
not import `QuotScheme`.  Project-local; blueprint `lem:openimm_beckchevalley` (mate). -/

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma pullbackSpecIso_hom_base :
    (pullbackSpecIso R S T).hom ≫ Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap R _)) =
      pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap _ _)) := by
  simp [Algebra.TensorProduct.algebraMap_def]

lemma isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout {A B C P : CommRingCat} (f : A ⟶ B) (g : A ⟶ C)
    (inl : B ⟶ P) (inr : C ⟶ P) (h : IsPushout f g inl inr) :
    IsPullback (Spec.map inl) (Spec.map inr) (Spec.map f) (Spec.map g) :=
  IsPullback.map Scheme.Spec h.op.flip

lemma isPullback_SpecMap_pushout {A B C : CommRingCat} (f : A ⟶ B) (g : A ⟶ C) :
    IsPullback (Spec.map (pushout.inl f g))
      (Spec.map (pushout.inr f g)) (Spec.map f) (Spec.map g) := by
  apply isPullback_SpecMap_of_isPushout
  exact IsPushout.of_hasPushout f g

lemma diagonal_SpecMap :
    pullback.diagonal (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R S))) =
      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (Algebra.TensorProduct.lmul' R : S ⊗[R] S →ₐ[R] S).toRingHom) ≫

data.content:
theorem of_horiz_isIso [IsIso fst] [IsIso g] (sq : CommSq fst snd f g) :
    IsPullback fst snd f g :=
  of_horiz_isIso_mono sq

lemma of_iso (h : IsPullback fst snd f g)
    {P' X' Y' Z' : C} {fst' : P' ⟶ X'} {snd' : P' ⟶ Y'} {f' : X' ⟶ Z'} {g' : Y' ⟶ Z'}
    (e₁ : P ≅ P') (e₂ : X ≅ X') (e₃ : Y ≅ Y') (e₄ : Z ≅ Z')
    (commfst : fst ≫ e₂.hom = e₁.hom ≫ fst')
    (commsnd : snd ≫ e₃.hom = e₁.hom ≫ snd')
    (commf : f ≫ e₄.hom = e₂.hom ≫ f')
    (commg : g ≫ e₄.hom = e₃.hom ≫ g') :
    IsPullback fst' snd' f' g' where
  w := by
    rw [← cancel_epi e₁.hom, ← reassoc_of% commfst, ← commf,
      ← reassoc_of% commsnd, ← commg, h.w_assoc]
  isLimit' :=
    ⟨(IsLimit.postcomposeInvEquiv
        (cospanExt e₂ e₃ e₄ commf.symm commg.symm) _).1
          (IsLimit.ofIsoLimit h.isLimit (by
            refine PullbackCone.ext e₁ ?_ ?_
            · change fst = e₁.hom ≫ fst' ≫ e₂.inv
              rw [← reassoc_of% commfst, e₂.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]
            · change snd = e₁.hom ≫ snd' ≫ e₃.inv
              rw [← reassoc_of% commsnd, e₃.hom_inv_id, Category.comp_id]))⟩

/-- Same as `IsPullback.of_iso`, but using the data and compatibilities involving
the inverse isomorphisms instead. -/
lemma of_iso' (h : IsPullback fst snd f g)
    {P' X' Y' Z' : C} {fst' : P' ⟶ X'} {snd' : P' ⟶ Y'} {f' : X' ⟶ Z'} {g' : Y' ⟶ Z'}
    (e₁ : P' ≅ P) (e₂ : X' ≅ X) (e₃ : Y' ≅ Y) (e₄ : Z' ≅ Z)
    (commfst : e₁.hom ≫ fst = fst' ≫ e₂.hom)
    (commsnd : e₁.hom ≫ snd = snd' ≫ e₃.hom)
    (commf : e₂.hom ≫ f = f' ≫ e₄.hom)
    (commg : e₃.hom ≫ g = g' ≫ e₄.hom) :
    IsPullback fst' snd' f' g' := by
  apply h.of_iso e₁.symm e₂.symm e₃.symm e₄.symm
  · simp only [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, ← commfst, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  · simp only [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, ← commsnd, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  · simp only [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, ← commf, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
  · simp only [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq, Category.assoc, ← commg, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]

section

variable {P X Y : C} {fst : P ⟶ X} {snd : P ⟶ X} {f : X ⟶ Y}

lemma isIso_fst_of_mono (h : IsPullback fst snd f f) (inst : Mono f := by infer_instance) :
    IsIso fst := h.cone.isIso_fst_of_mono_of_isLimit h.isLimit

lemma isIso_snd_iso_of_mono (h : IsPullback fst snd f f) (inst : Mono f := by infer_instance) :
    IsIso snd := h.cone.isIso_snd_of_mono_of_isLimit h.isLimit

end

section
The objects in the statement fit into the following diagram:
```
X₁₁ - h₁₁ -> X₁₂
|            |
v₁₁          v₁₂
↓            ↓
X₂₁ - h₂₁ -> X₂₂
|            |
v₂₁          v₂₂
↓            ↓
X₃₁ - h₃₁ -> X₃₂
```
-/
theorem paste_vert {X₁₁ X₁₂ X₂₁ X₂₂ X₃₁ X₃₂ : C} {h₁₁ : X₁₁ ⟶ X₁₂} {h₂₁ : X₂₁ ⟶ X₂₂}
    {h₃₁ : X₃₁ ⟶ X₃₂} {v₁₁ : X₁₁ ⟶ X₂₁} {v₁₂ : X₁₂ ⟶ X₂₂} {v₂₁ : X₂₁ ⟶ X₃₁} {v₂₂ : X₂₂ ⟶ X₃₂}
    (s : IsPullback h₁₁ v₁₁ v₁₂ h₂₁) (t : IsPullback h₂₁ v₂₁ v₂₂ h₃₁) :
    IsPullback h₁₁ (v₁₁ ≫ v₂₁) (v₁₂ ≫ v₂₂) h₃₁ :=
  of_isLimit (pasteHorizIsPullback rfl t.isLimit s.isLimit)

/-- Paste two pullback squares "horizontally" to obtain another pullback square.

The objects in the statement fit into the following diagram:
```
X₁₁ - h₁₁ -> X₁₂ - h₁₂ -> X₁₃
|            |            |
v₁₁          v₁₂          v₁₃
↓            ↓            ↓
X₂₁ - h₂₁ -> X₂₂ - h₂₂ -> X₂₃
```
-/
theorem paste_horiz {X₁₁ X₁₂ X₁₃ X₂₁ X₂₂ X₂₃ : C} {h₁₁ : X₁₁ ⟶ X₁₂} {h₁₂ : X₁₂ ⟶ X₁₃}
    {h₂₁ : X₂₁ ⟶ X₂₂} {h₂₂ : X₂₂ ⟶ X₂₃} {v₁₁ : X₁₁ ⟶ X₂₁} {v₁₂ : X₁₂ ⟶ X₂₂} {v₁₃ : X₁₃ ⟶ X₂₃}
    (s : IsPullback h₁₁ v₁₁ v₁₂ h₂₁) (t : IsPullback h₁₂ v₁₂ v₁₃ h₂₂) :
    IsPullback (h₁₁ ≫ h₁₂) v₁₁ v₁₃ (h₂₁ ≫ h₂₂) :=
  (paste_vert s.flip t.flip).flip

/-- Given a pullback square assembled from a commuting square on the top and
a pullback square on the bottom, the top square is a pullback square.

The objects in the statement fit into the following diagram:
```
namespace IsPullback
variable {P X Y Z : C} {fst : P ⟶ X} {snd : P ⟶ Y} {f : X ⟶ Z} {g : Y ⟶ Z}

theorem flip (h : IsPullback fst snd f g) : IsPullback snd fst g f :=
  of_isLimit (PullbackCone.flipIsLimit h.isLimit)

theorem flip_iff : IsPullback fst snd f g ↔ IsPullback snd fst g f :=
  ⟨flip, flip⟩


theorem op (h : IsPullback fst snd f g) : IsPushout g.op f.op snd.op fst.op :=
  IsPushout.of_isColimit
    (IsColimit.ofIsoColimit (Limits.PullbackCone.isLimitEquivIsColimitOp h.flip.cone h.flip.isLimit)
      h.toCommSq.flip.coneOp)

theorem unop {P X Y Z : Cᵒᵖ} {fst : P ⟶ X} {snd : P ⟶ Y} {f : X ⟶ Z} {g : Y ⟶ Z}
    (h : IsPullback fst snd f g) : IsPushout g.unop f.unop snd.unop fst.unop :=
  IsPushout.of_isColimit
    (IsColimit.ofIsoColimit
      (Limits.PullbackCone.isLimitEquivIsColimitUnop h.flip.cone h.flip.isLimit)
      h.toCommSq.flip.coneUnop)

end IsPullback

namespace IsPushout
variable {Z X Y P : C} {f : Z ⟶ X} {g : Z ⟶ Y} {inl : X ⟶ P} {inr : Y ⟶ P}

theorem flip (h : IsPushout f g inl inr) : IsPushout g f inr inl :=
  of_isColimit (PushoutCocone.flipIsColimit h.isColimit)

theorem flip_iff : IsPushout f g inl inr ↔ IsPushout g f inr inl :=
  ⟨flip, flip⟩

theorem op (h : IsPushout f g inl inr) : IsPullback inr.op inl.op g.op f.op :=
  IsPullback.of_isLimit
    (IsLimit.ofIsoLimit
      (Limits.PushoutCocone.isColimitEquivIsLimitOp h.flip.cocone h.flip.isColimit)
      h.toCommSq.flip.coconeOp)

theorem unop {Z X Y P : Cᵒᵖ} {f : Z ⟶ X} {g : Z ⟶ Y} {inl : X ⟶ P} {inr : Y ⟶ P}
    (h : IsPushout f g inl inr) : IsPullback inr.unop inl.unop g.unop f.unop :=

· simp

alias IsPullback.map := Functor.map_isPullback

alias IsPushout.map := Functor.map_isPushout

theorem IsPullback.of_map [ReflectsLimit (cospan h i) F] (e : f ≫ h = g ≫ i)
    (H : IsPullback (F.map f) (F.map g) (F.map h) (F.map i)) : IsPullback f g h i := by
  refine ⟨⟨e⟩, ⟨isLimitOfReflects F <| ?_⟩⟩
  refine
    (IsLimit.equivOfNatIsoOfIso (cospanCompIso F h i) _ _ (WalkingCospan.ext ?_ ?_ ?_)).symm
      H.isLimit
  exacts [Iso.refl _, (Category.comp_id _).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm,
    (Category.comp_id _).trans (Category.id_comp _).symm]

theorem IsPullback.of_map_of_faithful [ReflectsLimit (cospan h i) F] [F.Faithful]
    (H : IsPullback (F.map f) (F.map g) (F.map h) (F.map i)) : IsPullback f g h i :=
  H.of_map F (F.map_injective <| by simpa only [F.map_comp] using H.w)

theorem IsPullback.map_iff {D : Type*} [Category* D] (F : C ⥤ D) [PreservesLimit (cospan h i) F]
    [ReflectsLimit (cospan h i) F] (e : f ≫ h = g ≫ i) :

def openCoverOfLeft (𝒰 : OpenCover.{v} X) (f : X ⟶ Z) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
    OpenCover (pullback f g) where
  I₀ := 𝒰.I₀
  X i := pullback (𝒰.f i ≫ f) g
  f i := pullback.map (𝒰.f i ≫ f) g f g (𝒰.f i) (𝟙 Y) (𝟙 Z) (by simp) (by simp)
  mem₀ := by
    rw [ofArrows_mem_precoverage_iff]
    refine ⟨fun x ↦ ?_, fun i ↦ ?_⟩
    · letI 𝒱 := ((gluing 𝒰.ulift f g).openCover.pushforwardIso
              (limit.isoLimitCone ⟨_, gluedIsLimit 𝒰.ulift f g⟩).inv).copy
          𝒰.ulift.I₀ (fun i => pullback (𝒰.ulift.f i ≫ f) g)
          (fun i => pullback.map _ _ _ _ (𝒰.ulift.f i) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (Category.comp_id _) (by simp))
          (Equiv.refl 𝒰.ulift.I₀) (fun _ => Iso.refl _) fun i ↦ by
        simp_rw [Cover.pushforwardIso_I₀, Cover.pushforwardIso_f, GlueData.openCover_f,
          GlueData.openCover_I₀, gluing_J]
        exact pullback.hom_ext (by simp [p1]) (by simp [p2])
      obtain ⟨i, x, rfl⟩ := 𝒱.exists_eq x
      exact ⟨_, x, rfl⟩
    · dsimp

(h : ∀ (X : C), ∃ (A B : C) (p : A ⟶ B) (_ : P p) (_ : B ⟶ X), IsIso p) :
    isomorphisms C ≤ P.pushouts := by
  intro X Y f (_ : IsIso f)
  obtain ⟨A, B, p, hp, g, _⟩ := h X
  exact ⟨A, B, p, p ≫ g, g ≫ f, hp, (IsPushout.of_id_snd (f := p ≫ g)).of_iso
    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _) (asIso p) (asIso f) (by simp) (by simp) (by simp) (by simp)⟩

/-- A morphism property is `IsStableUnderBaseChange` if the base change of such a morphism
still falls in the class. -/
class IsStableUnderBaseChange : Prop where
  of_isPullback {X Y Y' S : C} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : Y ⟶ S} {f' : Y' ⟶ Y} {g' : Y' ⟶ X}
    (sq : IsPullback f' g' g f) (hg : P g) : P g'

instance : P.pullbacks.IsStableUnderBaseChange where
  of_isPullback := by
    rintro _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ h ⟨_, _, _, _, _, hp, hq⟩
    exact P.pullbacks_mk (h.paste_horiz hq) hp

/-- A morphism property is `IsStableUnderCobaseChange` if the cobase change of such a morphism
still falls in the class. -/
class IsStableUnderCobaseChange : Prop where
  of_isPushout {A A' B B' : C} {f : A ⟶ A'} {g : A ⟶ B} {f' : B ⟶ B'} {g' : A' ⟶ B'}
    (sq : IsPushout g f f' g') (hf : P f) : P f'

instance : P.pushouts.IsStableUnderCobaseChange where
  of_isPushout := by
    rintro _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ h ⟨_, _, _, _, _, hp, hq⟩
    exact P.pushouts_mk (hq.paste_horiz h) hp

/-- `P.HasPullbacksAlong f` states that for any morphism satisfying `P` with the same codomain
as `f`, the pullback of that morphism along `f` exists. -/
protected class HasPullbacksAlong {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  hasPullback {W} (g : W ⟶ Y) : P g → HasPullback g f

instance {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) [HasPullbacksAlong f] : P.HasPullbacksAlong f where
  hasPullback _ _ := inferInstance

/-- `P.HasPushoutsAlong f` states that for any morphism satisfying `P` with the same domain
as `f`, the pushout of that morphism along `f` exists. -/
protected class HasPushoutsAlong {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  hasPushout {W} (g : X ⟶ W) : P g → HasPushout g f

instance {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) [HasPushoutsAlong f] : P.HasPushoutsAlong f where
  hasPushout _ _ := inferInstance

/-- `P.IsStableUnderBaseChangeAlong f` states that for any morphism satisfying `P` with the same
codomain as `f`, any pullback of that morphism along `f` also satisfies `P`. -/
class IsStableUnderBaseChangeAlong {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  of_isPullback {Z W : C} {f' : W ⟶ Z} {g' : W ⟶ X} {g : Z ⟶ Y}
    (pb : IsPullback f' g' g f) : P g → P g'

instance [P.IsStableUnderBaseChange] {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : P.IsStableUnderBaseChangeAlong f where
  of_isPullback := IsStableUnderBaseChange.of_isPullback

/-- `P.IsStableUnderCobaseChangeAlong f` states that for any morphism satisfying `P` with the same
codomain as `f`, any pullback of that morphism along `f` also satisfies `P`. -/
class IsStableUnderCobaseChangeAlong {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
  of_isPushout {Z W : C} {f' : Z ⟶ W} {g' : Y ⟶ W} {g : X ⟶ Z}
    (pb : IsPushout f g g' f') : P g → P g'
